message:
chore(beads): review-pass-12 — 2 HIGH CI-parallelism + hook-distribution beads

Deeper CI / workflow audit. Each bead verified via grep / ls /
git config inspection before filing (no speculation after the
pass-10 s5vn calibration-down lesson).

  frankenpandas-0iyb [HIGH] ci: no concurrency: cancel-in-progress
                            group in any workflow. Every rapid
                            push triggers a duplicate full-run
                            CI cycle; supersededlanding runs
                            consume ~7 min each. At swarm velocity
                            (20+ commits/day), 140 min/day of
                            runner time spent on work-that's-already-
                            been-superseded. 3-line fix:
                              concurrency:
                                group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
                                cancel-in-progress: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' }}

  frankenpandas-pa2y [HIGH] workflow: git hooks live in
                            .git/hooks/hooks.d/ (local-only, never
                            cloned). Fresh contributor / agent has
                            ZERO swarm-coordination hooks. The
                            iy8u-doctrine (diff-stat + co-landed
                            bead IDs) is aspirational rather than
                            enforced because the hook that's
                            supposed to enforce it doesn't travel
                            with the repo. Proposes: commit hooks
                            as ./githooks/, add scripts/install-hooks.sh
                            that sets core.hooksPath, document the
                            bootstrap in AGENTS.md + CONTRIBUTING.md,
                            add server-side CI enforcement as
                            belt-and-suspenders.

Interlock with prior passes:
- 0iyb (concurrency group) + ffhs (CI job splitting) + 0l5r
  (cross-platform matrix) + kmbc (feature matrix) + 36qc
  (cargo-audit job) + hg60 (secret scan job) — six CI-shape
  beads converge on the ci.yml rewrite. They should land as
  a single coordinated refactor or a tight series of PRs;
  otherwise each new job inherits the wasteful-duplicate-run
  problem that 0iyb fixes.

- pa2y (hook commit) + 6d5s (CONTRIBUTING.md) + iy8u-doctrine
  (closed) + hg60 (secret scan hook) + thty (rustfmt pre-commit
  hook) — five workflow beads converge on the ./githooks/
  directory structure. pa2y ships the scaffolding; the other
  four populate it.

Running session totals after 12 review passes:
  2 CRITICAL (d6xa closed)
  39 HIGH (8 closed via swarm; 31 open)
  5 MEDIUM
  1 LOW
= 47 review beads total.

Calibration note: every bead in pass-11 and pass-12 was
verified via direct grep / ls / git config before filing.
No speculative sizing (s5vn's miscount was an isolated
pass-10 issue).

Workspace 3171/0/13, 0 clippy warnings. Zero shipped-code
bugs across all 12 passes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

+{"id":"frankenpandas-0iyb","title":"[HIGH] ci: no concurrency: cancel-in-progress group — rapid PR pushes trigger duplicate CI runs (5-8x waste per PR)","description":"CI concurrency audit.

**Observation.** `.github/workflows/*.yml` — zero matches for `concurrency:` or `cancel-in-progress:`.

**What this means.** When an author pushes commit A to a PR branch, then pushes commit B 30 seconds later (fixup, rebase, etc.), GitHub Actions triggers TWO runs: one for A, one for B. Both fully run through all jobs (checks, fuzz-regression, etc.) even though only run B's result is relevant.

**Swarm-specific amplification.** Today's git log shows 20+ commits across 6 hours from multiple agents (cc-pandas + Dicklesworthstone + parallel workers + auto-rebase workflows). Every session with multiple rapid commits doubles, triples, or quadruples the CI load for no additional signal. br-0848138's `br flush` commit + my `pass-11` commit would trigger 2 runs; the `br flush` result doesn't matter because pass-11 is newer.

Rough math:
- Single PR with typical 5-8 commits: without concurrency group → 5-8 full CI runs (5-8 × ~7 min = 35-56 runner minutes).
- With concurrency group → only the final push's run completes (~7 min).
- Ratio: 5-8x waste.
- Swarm has ~20 commits/day × 7 min/run = 140 min/day CI cycles spent on superseded work.

**The standard fix.** Three lines at the top of each workflow:
```yaml
concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true
```

`group` identifies "same workflow, same branch/PR"; `cancel-in-progress: true` cancels the still-running older run when a new push lands. Exception on the main branch: use `cancel-in-progress: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' }}` so pushes to main never cancel each other (main branch runs are archival).

**Severity.** HIGH — compound cost across every PR in perpetuity. At this swarm's velocity, easily the cheapest bead in the backlog to pay off.

**Fix shape:**

1. Add `concurrency:` block to `.github/workflows/ci.yml` at workflow level:
   ```yaml
   name: CI
   on: [push, pull_request]

   concurrency:
     group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
     cancel-in-progress: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' }}

   jobs:
     checks:
       ...
   ```

2. If any future workflow is added (br-4clx release workflow, br-36qc security workflow, br-ing6 nightly perf), each needs its own `concurrency:` group — or share the `group:` so they serialize / cancel together.

3. Consider a tighter group key for PR runs:
   ```yaml
   concurrency:
     group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref }}
     cancel-in-progress: true
   ```
   The `pull_request.number || github.ref` idiom groups by PR number on PR events (always unique per PR) and by ref on direct pushes. Belt-and-suspenders version.

Blast radius: ci.yml + any future workflow file, 3-4 lines each. Zero source changes, zero test changes.

Prerequisites: None. Ship in isolation.

**Related:**
- br-ffhs (CI job splitting) — split jobs each need their own `concurrency:` group OR share one. Coordinate the two PRs.
- br-0l5r (cross-platform matrix) — matrix jobs should ALSO inherit the concurrency cancel so one matrix cell doesn't get canceled while another proceeds (generally fine with the above — whole workflow cancels).
- br-4clx (release workflow) — ships a new workflow, needs its own concurrency block.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T21:24:46.716318082Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-22T21:24:46.716318082Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","concurrency","review","waste"]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-0jmn","title":"pandas: Series.reset_index(name=...) value-column naming parity","description":"Python signature: pd.Series.reset_index(level=None, drop=False, name=..., inplace=False, allow_duplicates=<no_default>). Current fp-frame Series.reset_index only accepts drop: bool and cannot override the value column name on the DataFrame-return path. Implement the name override for single-index Series, preserve the existing drop=True Series branch, and add focused tests for named series, unnamed series, and collision handling with index/index-name columns.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:59:59.749325004Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T18:45:26.544815036Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T18:45:26.543000433Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-0kb3","title":"fp-columnar Column.astype parity","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T06:59:50.285667679Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T07:10:15.788534132Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T07:10:15.786415942Z","close_reason":"Implemented Column::astype in commit 88836d2, 5 tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-0l5r","title":"[HIGH] ci: ubuntu-latest only — macOS/Windows cross-platform claim unverified","description":"Cross-platform CI audit.

**Observation.** .github/workflows/ci.yml line 10: `runs-on: ubuntu-latest`. No `strategy.matrix` defining a per-OS test matrix. No macOS runner. No Windows runner.

**Implications.**
- fp-io reads/writes Excel (.xlsx, .xls), CSV, Parquet, Feather, Arrow IPC. CSV line-ending semantics (LF vs CRLF) differ across OS. Excel / Parquet zip-archive handling via calamine / parquet-crate depends on OS-level file layer on some paths. None of this is tested on Windows, where the failure modes would appear.
- Path separators: fp-io has pub fn read_csv(path: &Path), pub fn read_parquet(path: &Path), etc. On Windows, a fixture path string produced on Linux with "/" may or may not resolve correctly depending on canonicalization. Not tested.
- Endianness: arrow / parquet byte manipulation is all little-endian; future big-endian fixtures or a PowerPC build would regress silently.
- The README advertises "Cross-platform (Linux/macOS/Windows)" under Installation implicitly by describing standard cargo build. Nothing validates that claim.

**Severity.** HIGH — product claim (cross-platform Rust library) is unverified. A Windows user of frankenpandas today is running untested code on every IO surface.

**Fix shape:**

ci.yml gains:
```
jobs:
  checks:
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        os: [ubuntu-latest, macos-latest, windows-latest]
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    ...
```

Caveats and cost:
- Windows runners are ~2x slower than Ubuntu. Initial run will take ~12 min instead of 6.
- rch (remote-build helper) likely won't exist on Windows runners. Windows CI would build locally via `cargo test --workspace`.
- cargo-llvm-cov (installed but unused per sibling bead) works on macOS, limited on Windows; Windows job may need to skip the install.
- Some tests may rely on POSIX behavior: `std::fs::set_permissions` chmod bits, symlinks, etc. Expect 0-5 failures on the first Windows run that need per-OS cfg-gating or fixture adjustments. These become trackable beads.
- Excel path resolution on Windows uses \ separators; calamine's `open_workbook` may handle this but needs verification.

Incremental path if 3-OS matrix is too aggressive at once:
1. Start: ubuntu + macos (rch-compatible POSIX).
2. Next: add windows with only a `cargo check` + `cargo test -p fp-types -p fp-columnar -p fp-index` (no IO) to surface path-handling bugs without full IO breakage.
3. Finally: full `cargo test --workspace --all-targets` on windows.

Related: per-OS arrow/parquet/calamine versions don't diverge today (one workspace.dependencies block) — so any divergence in test behavior is a code bug, not a dep bug.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T19:59:30.233193403Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-22T19:59:30.233193403Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","cross-platform","review"]}

The test should verify non-null counting semantics per group and per time bucket, plus the resulting row ordering and group key materialization.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-22T04:28:34.974226456Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T04:44:23.565591092Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T04:44:23.563598490Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-p762","title":"[MEDIUM] fuzz: add fuzz_pivot_table_bytes target (reshape + agg dispatch surface)","description":"Reviewing fuzz/fuzz_targets/ inventory: 14 targets cover IO + scalar/dtype + low-level groupby/series ops, but pivot_table is uncovered despite being a high-complexity reshape surface that recently had multiple parity bugs (frankenpandas-7n5h aggfunc dict, FP-P2D-127 unsupported_agg fixture stale, FP-P2D-128 reshape packet failure, plus pivot_table_with_margins/_multi_values/_with_margins_name variants).

Proposed target: fuzz_pivot_table.rs that:
1. Splits input bytes via dual-mode envelope (raw vs synth, like fuzz_parquet_io).
2. Synth mode: project bytes into a small DataFrame with 2 categorical key columns + 1 numeric value column (4-8 rows). Pick aggfunc from a fixed set {sum, mean, count, min, max, prod, median, var, std, first}.
3. Calls frame.pivot_table(values, index, columns, aggfunc); panics when allowed (catch_unwind?), otherwise returns Ok/Err normally.
4. For successful pivots, asserts shape invariants: cell count == |unique_index| × |unique_columns|; aggregated values are not NaN unless source was missing.

New fp-conformance pub fn fuzz_pivot_table_bytes(input: &[u8]) -> Result<(), FpIoError> following the existing fuzz_excel_io_bytes / fuzz_parquet_io_bytes shape; new fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_pivot_table.rs and Cargo.toml [[bin]] entry.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:23:38.029372224Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:53:21.078923680Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:53:21.077084415Z","close_reason":"Completed in 577f883","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-p90t","title":"Add series str.islower/isupper unicode-symbol strict conformance packets","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-19T01:28:22.482947011Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T01:33:23.897879093Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T01:33:23.895991752Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-pa2y","title":"[HIGH] workflow: git hooks live in .git/hooks (not committed) — fresh clones have zero swarm-coordination enforcement","description":"Git-hooks distribution audit.

**Observation.**
- `.git/hooks/hooks.d/pre-commit/50-agent-mail.py` and `pre-push/50-agent-mail.py` exist as the swarm's coordination hooks.
- `.git/hooks/` is git-local — lives inside `.git/`, which `git clone` does NOT reproduce. A fresh clone gets git's stock sample hooks only.
- No committed `.githooks/` directory at repo root.
- No `core.hooksPath` config set (verified: grep of .git/config returns nothing).
- No `scripts/install-hooks.sh` or equivalent bootstrap script.
- AGENTS.md doesn't mention hook installation.

**Consequence.** When a new contributor / agent clones the repo:
- They have ZERO pre-commit hooks. The agent-mail coordination never runs.
- Their commits push directly to the branch without the swarm coordination the existing hooks enforce.
- The doctrine captured by br-iy8u (diff-stat requirement, co-landed bead IDs) is supposed to be enforced by pre-commit hooks. Without committed hooks, the doctrine is aspirational.
- My own pass-1 through pass-11 commits never triggered the agent-mail hook because this fresh cc-pandas session also has a fresh .git/ that may or may not have the hook. (I was told the hooks were in place; my git commits succeeded, so either the hook passed or it's not actually enforced on this workspace. Uncertain.)

**Industry pattern: commit the hooks + use `core.hooksPath`.**

1. Move `.git/hooks/hooks.d/` → `./githooks/` (committed to repo).
2. Commit the existing 50-agent-mail.py + any future hooks under `githooks/pre-commit/` and `githooks/pre-push/`.
3. Add `scripts/install-hooks.sh`:
   ```bash
   #!/bin/sh
   set -euo pipefail
   git config core.hooksPath "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/githooks"
   echo "hooks installed at $(git config core.hooksPath)"
   ```
4. AGENTS.md + CONTRIBUTING.md (br-6d5s) add: "First clone? Run `./scripts/install-hooks.sh` to set up swarm coordination hooks."
5. Optional: a post-clone helper that exits non-zero from CI if commits lack the hook-enforced co-landed-bead-id footer.

Alternatively, adopt the `pre-commit` framework (pre-commit.com):
- `.pre-commit-config.yaml` describes hooks.
- Contributors run `pre-commit install` once; the framework installs the hooks into `.git/hooks/` via a standard convention.
- Pros: widely adopted, cached, cross-language, can run hooks on changed files only.
- Cons: Python dependency, adds a bootstrap step.

**Skill rule / swarm-doctrine.** br-iy8u captured the doctrine that commits MUST have `git diff --stat` + co-landed bead IDs. The only mechanical enforcement of that doctrine is the 50-agent-mail.py hook. Without committing the hook, the doctrine is a README note rather than a guardrail.

**Severity.** HIGH — enforcement gap. Any agent or human whose fresh clone doesn't run install-hooks.sh (and none exists) can sidestep the swarm doctrine silently. Compounds with br-6d5s (CODEOWNERS / CONTRIBUTING.md) — those beads assume a contributor onboarding flow that doesn't exist yet.

**Fix shape:**

1. Commit existing hooks:
   `git mv .git/hooks/hooks.d githooks` (literally — or `cp` + `git add` since .git/hooks is git-local).
   Review the hooks for any hardcoded paths that need relativizing.
2. `scripts/install-hooks.sh` sets `core.hooksPath`.
3. AGENTS.md update: mention the install step as part of the initial-workspace-setup ritual.
4. CONTRIBUTING.md (br-6d5s blocker) — one sentence: "after cloning, run `./scripts/install-hooks.sh`."
5. CI guard: enforce in ci.yml that every commit's message contains either `br-frankenpandas-*` OR `co-authored-by` OR the commit is explicitly a merge. Fails PR on any commit that lacks the swarm-doctrine footer. Gives the same enforcement server-side if a contributor forgot to install hooks locally.

Blast radius:
- 1 new directory at repo root (`githooks/`), + ~2 files (~100 lines each — the existing agent-mail.py).
- 1 new script (~15 lines).
- AGENTS.md + CONTRIBUTING.md additions (~20 lines each).
- CI.yml addition for server-side enforcement (~20 lines).

Zero source-code changes. Hook behavior itself is preserved from today's state.

Prerequisites: None urgent; can ship independently. br-6d5s (CONTRIBUTING.md) is a natural pair.

**Related:**
- br-6d5s (CODEOWNERS / PR template / CONTRIBUTING.md) — this bead fills in the hook-bootstrap piece.
- br-iy8u (workflow doctrine, closed) — the doctrine this bead operationalizes.
- br-hg60 (secret scanning) — secret-scan hooks belong in the committed githooks/ dir too.
- br-thty (rustfmt config) — suggested pre-commit fmt hook goes in this bead's githooks/ structure.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T21:25:34.906992890Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-22T21:25:34.906992890Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["hooks","onboarding","review","workflow"]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-pavu","title":"[HIGH] fuzz: cross-format round-trip parity fuzz target (catch IO+Equality drift)","description":"Existing fuzz harness has individual format round-trip targets (excel/parquet/feather/ipc_stream/json/csv) but each only checks 'parse → write → reparse → equals'. None of them check CROSS-FORMAT parity: parquet write of a frame should produce data that, when written through arrow IPC, contains the same logical values. Recent IO drift bugs (br-989b excel index leak, br-mxho fuzz excel leak, plus calamine 0.34.0 dependency bump) suggest format-specific assumptions can silently diverge.

Proposed target: fuzz_format_cross_round_trip.rs:
1. Synth mode: project bytes into a small DataFrame (3-8 rows, 2-3 typed columns).
2. Pick a primary format (parquet/feather/ipc_stream) from input bytes.
3. Round-trip through primary format → frame_a.
4. Round-trip through a SECONDARY format → frame_b.
5. Assert frame_a.semantic_eq(frame_b) (cross-format invariant: the lossy bits of any individual format must converge to the same logical content).
6. Assert no panic on any byte input.

New fp-conformance pub fn fuzz_format_cross_round_trip_bytes(input: &[u8]). Catches the entire class of drift where format X loses metadata that format Y preserves.","notes":"cc-pandas triage 2026-04-22: implemented in fuzz_targets/fuzz_format_cross_round_trip.rs + fp_conformance::fuzz_format_cross_round_trip_bytes (lib.rs:5138). Synthesizes a frame, round-trips through a primary FuzzArrowFormat, then through a distinct secondary format, asserts frame-level parity via DataFrame::equals. Landed in commit 49f0cdf.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:24:44.631990108Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:33:49.204576948Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:33:49.201998451Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-pbh9","title":"fp-columnar Column.fillna_with_column parity","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:30:42.323915804Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T08:35:39.046032956Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T08:35:39.044217778Z","close_reason":"Implemented Column::fillna_with_column in commit 178e515, 1 test","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-pc4","title":"Add DataFrame GroupBy.get_group conformance test coverage","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-17T08:31:51.516284535Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-17T08:33:35.852135053Z","closed_at":"2026-04-17T08:33:35.849856219Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
